page: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-64 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the n4d server family. It features 64 dedicated virtual CPUs and 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This resource ratio positions the instance within the general-purpose category, designed to handle workloads requiring a steady balance of compute and memory resources.

The instance does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached storage options. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this configuration. Due to the lack of specific benchmark data, performance characteristics are defined by its dedicated physical resource allocation. This server is suitable for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded applications, web serving, and enterprise applications that scale across 64 vCPUs without requiring specialized hardware acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-64 is a general-purpose server instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on external storage solutions. With its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced resource distribution, this server is designed for general-purpose enterprise workloads, web serving, and multi-threaded applications that do not require specialized hardware acceleration.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-64 is a general-purpose server featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB of RAM on an x86_64 architecture, with no bundled local storage or GPUs.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose server configuration featuring sixty-four dedicated virtual cores and two hundred fifty-six gigabytes of system memory.

No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included
Designed for balanced general-purpose and multi-threaded enterprise workloads
